dln38nikceaig7v2i4vbq9i350.summary: No past meeting summaries with Kathleen Ores Walsh exist in your notes — the prior context available covers SMS architecture, business architecture maturation, and the Architecture of Architecture business case with other stakeholders (Matt, Jonathan, Brian). The prep instructions ask you to review sent emails and come ready to speak to the future of underwriting and an AI use case for underwriting — neither topic surfaces in your saved notes, so your prep hinges entirely on that email review. Treat this as a fresh context-build with Kathleen until prior meeting notes are captured.
tasks: Review your sent email history with Kathleen Ores Walsh and extract any threads touching underwriting strategy, AI, or architecture decisions before the meeting.
Formulate a clear point of view on the future of underwriting — identify whether the strategic direction leans toward automation, risk model modernization, data centralization, or some combination.

Define the specific AI use case for underwriting you plan to present — name the capability, the business problem it solves, and how it connects to one or more of the five core biometrics (retention, cost to serve, etc.) your architecture practice already anchors to.

Determine what — if anything — from the Unified Enterprise Messaging Fabric or Architecture of Architecture work is relevant to an underwriting AI use case, in case Kathleen draws a cross-domain line.
Decide what you need from Kathleen out of this sync — a decision, an alignment, a resource commitment — so you can drive to a concrete next step rather than leaving with an open thread.
dln38nikceaig7v2i4vbq9i350.title: Architecture Sync with Kathleen Ores Walsh

neo1tsbhd6a4oke2sq0f50ep8c.summary: You and Praveena are mid-stream on a major operational model effort — specifically the products and services catalog, where Praveena completed pre-work as of 5/6 and was awaiting SME feedback to finalize content. Running threads include the executive workshop narrative around architecture value, the AI requirements automation tool needing industrialization, and organizational anxiety around leadership transitions (Boucher out, Deepak and Dan in). Today's prep instructions point to two things: horizontal planning needs and a review of product/service feedback — both map directly to the catalog work and the Phase 2 effectiveness deep-dives Praveena has been driving.
tasks: Review the current state of the products and services catalog — confirm whether SME feedback has been collected and what gaps remain before the content can be finalized.
Identify what 'horizontal planning' requires from Praveena's workstream — determine which services, domains, or deliverables need cross-team visibility or coordination inputs.
Pull together any feedback received on architecture products and services since 5/6 — surface patterns Deepak or other leaders have flagged as missing or underdeveloped.
Check the status of the domain portfolio spreadsheet last reviewed 4/27 — confirm whether the proposed dotted-box domains (DNS/MVO and Transformation/TVO) have been validated or remain theoretical.

Be ready to discuss how the AI requirements automation prototype fits into the formal catalog — decide whether it surfaces as a new service entry and how to route it through Chuck without creating friction.
neo1tsbhd6a4oke2sq0f50ep8c.title: Bi-weekly 1:1 w/Praveena | Mark (series ends 1/4/27)
